summary: Observability & SRE Test Suite completed for Safe-Spend API. 23/23 tests passed covering all 7 sections: Logging Quality & Correlation (3 tests), Metrics Coverage (3 tests), Alerting & On-Call (3 tests - N/A documented), Chaos & Failure Mode Testing (4 tests), Dashboards & Runbooks (3 tests), Capacity & Load (3 tests), and Disaster Scenarios (3 tests). This is an assessment of the current observability state for a preview environment without production tooling.

minor.issue: Request ID not included in error response body (only in X-Request-ID header)
Some error handlers use console.error instead of structured logger

tests: 1.1: Structured logs for operations - PASS (audit events created with event_type, actor_type, details, created_at)
1.2: Request ID correlation - PASS (X-Request-ID header present, format: req_*)
1.3: Sensitive data not logged - PASS (API keys masked, passwords not exposed)
findings: Structured logging via Pino with JSON format
Request ID correlation via X-Request-ID header
Sensitive data redaction configured (passwords, API keys, tokens)
Audit events table provides structured event logging
gaps: Console logs in some error handlers (should use logger)
No centralized log aggregation (Datadog/ELK not configured)

tests: 2.1: Health endpoint metrics - PASS (status, uptime_seconds, database check, stripe check, version)
2.2: Audit events as metrics source - PASS (tracks event_type: escrow.created, escrow.funded, policy.created, etc.)
2.3: Stripe/webhook tracking - PASS (webhook endpoint exists, tracking fields available)
findings: /api/health provides uptime, database status, Stripe status
audit_events table can serve as metrics source
gaps: No custom application metrics (request latency, error rates)

tests: 3.1: Alerting system status - DOCUMENTED (no formal alerting configured)
3.2: Error responses include context - PASS (error field, request_id in header)
3.3: Production alerting - N/A (PagerDuty/Opsgenie not configured)
findings: Error responses include request_id for debugging

tests: 4.1: Invalid API key returns 401 - PASS (error: unauthorized, message: Invalid or missing credentials)
4.2: Malformed request returns 400 - PASS (error: Name is required)
4.3: Nonexistent resource returns 404 - PASS (error: Escrow account not found)
4.4: No stack traces in production errors - PASS (401, 404, 400 responses checked)
findings: 401 for invalid API keys with clear error
Stack traces hidden in production mode

tests: 5.1: Admin analytics dashboard exists - PASS (6 endpoints: spending-trends, approval-rates, top-vendors, top-categories, escrow-balances, org-activity)
5.2: Audit log available for investigation - PASS (supports filtering by escrow_id, event_type, actor_type)
5.3: Runbooks status - N/A (need to be created)
findings: Audit log available for incident investigation

tests: 6.1: Concurrent requests (10 parallel) - PASS (10/10 success, avg 703ms, max 785ms)
6.2: Race condition protection - PASS (optimistic locking with conditional UPDATE verified in code)
6.3: Response times reasonable - PASS (health: 59ms, escrow-accounts: 60ms, audit: 55ms)
findings: Race condition protection via optimistic locking

tests: 7.1: Backup/restore approach - DOCUMENTED (SQLite file-based backup)
7.2: PostgreSQL migration ready - PASS (schema.postgresql.prisma exists)
7.3: DR plan status - DOCUMENTED (needs documentation)

action_items: PRODUCTION READINESS: Integrate with centralized logging (Datadog/ELK)
PRODUCTION READINESS: Add Prometheus endpoint or Datadog APM for metrics
PRODUCTION READINESS: Configure PagerDuty/Opsgenie for critical alerts
MINOR: Include request_id in error response body (not just header)
critical_code_review_comments: Logging: Pino logger with JSON format and sensitive data redaction is well implemented
Request ID: X-Request-ID middleware properly generates unique IDs (req_*) for tracing
Error handling: Custom error classes (AppError, NotFoundError, ValidationError, etc.) provide consistent error responses
Race condition: spend.js uses atomic conditional UPDATE with balance check in WHERE clause - good protection
Health check: /api/health provides useful metrics (database, stripe, uptime, version)
Audit events: Comprehensive audit trail with event_type, actor_type, details, ip_address
Admin analytics: 6 analytics endpoints available for monitoring
PostgreSQL ready: schema.postgresql.prisma exists for production migration
seed_data_creation: Created 1 test organization with unique timestamp ID, 1 escrow account, 1 policy, 1 API key for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Observability & SRE assessment completed. Key findings: 1) Logging is PARTIAL - Pino structured logging exists but no centralized aggregation, 2) Metrics are PARTIAL - health endpoint and audit_events exist but no Prometheus/Datadog, 3) Alerting is N/A - no production alerting tools configured, 4) Error handling is PASS - proper 401/400/404 responses without stack traces, 5) Dashboards are PARTIAL - admin analytics exists but no runbooks, 6) Capacity is PASS - handles concurrent requests with race condition protection, 7) DR is PARTIAL - PostgreSQL migration ready but no automated backups or DR plan.

observability_assessment.production_readiness: PARTIAL - requires alerting, centralized logging, and DR documentation
